edges.description: EMD-associated cardiac emerinopathy can present with progressive atrial standstill and left ventricular noncompaction.
Progressive atrial myopathy culminates in failure of atrial electrical activity.
Scarred atrial myocardium loses recordable electrical activity and pacing capture.
Progressive atrial structural remodeling can dilate the right atrium in advanced atrial standstill.
Progressive atrial structural remodeling can dilate the left atrium in advanced atrial standstill.
Electrical silence of atrial myocardium is the defining electrophysiologic component of atrial standstill.
Failure of atrial depolarization removes atrial P waves from the surface ECG.
Absent atrial depolarization eliminates coordinated atrial contraction.
When atrial activity is absent, cardiac output depends on junctional or ventricular escape rhythms.
Ineffective atrial contraction can promote stasis and thromboembolic stroke risk, especially in genetic cardiomyopathy contexts.
Increased thromboembolic risk can manifest clinically as ischemic stroke.
Dependence on slow escape rhythms manifests clinically as bradycardia.
Slow escape rhythm or pauses can reduce cerebral perfusion enough to cause presyncope or syncope.

evidence.snippet: Atrial standstill (AS), a rare arrhythmogenic condition, is defined by (1) the absence of P waves in surface and intracavitary electrocardiograms (ECGs), (2) the absence of A waves in jugular venous pulse and right atrial pressure tracings, (3) the presence of a supraventricular type QRS complex, (4) the immobility of the atria on fluoroscopy, and (5) the inability to stimulate the atria electrically.
